description:
You are acting as a junior backup-administrator finishing today’s shift.  
Inside the workstation you will find one text file that another admin left for you:

• /home/user/backup/files_to_backup.txt – A plain-text list of absolute paths, one per line.  
  The list is unsorted and contains duplicates.

Your job is to turn that list into the canonical manifest that the nightly tape job expects.

Required end-state:

1. Create a new file called  
   /home/user/backup/files_to_backup.sorted.txt

2. The new file must meet ALL of these rules exactly:  
   a) Contain every line that appears at least once in the original list.  
   b) No line may be repeated.  
   c) Lines must be ordered lexicographically (the same order produced by the GNU
      `sort` utility with no extra options other than “unique” handling).  
   d) The file must end with exactly one trailing newline and **no extra blank lines**.

   The automated checker will compare the entire file byte-for-byte against its
   expected reference, so spacing, order and the final newline must be perfect.

3. Append a single log entry to  
   /home/user/backup/operation.log  
   The entry must be exactly (including the period and newline at the end):

   files_to_backup.sorted.txt generated.

   Make sure you append, not overwrite: if the log file is already present,
   your line must be added as the very last line; if it does not yet exist
   you must create it.

Constraints:

• Accomplish steps 1–3 with a single logical shell command (pipes and
  redirections are fine; using “;” or “&&” to chain sub-commands in one line is
  also acceptable).  
• Do not move, rename, or delete the original files_to_backup.txt file.  
• You have normal user privileges; assume /home/user/backup/ is writable.  

The automated tests will verify BOTH files exactly as described, so be precise.
truth:
Initial files and their contents before the task starts:

1. Path: /home/user/backup/files_to_backup.txt  
   Content (including newlines, no leading/trailing spaces):
/etc/hosts
/home/user/data/report1.csv
/etc/hosts
/var/log/syslog
/home/user/data/report2.csv
/var/log/syslog
<EOF>

2. The file /home/user/backup/operation.log is absent at start-up.

Expected files after the agent finishes:

1. /home/user/backup/files_to_backup.sorted.txt  
   Exact byte-for-byte content:
 /etc/hosts
/home/user/data/report1.csv
/home/user/data/report2.csv
/var/log/syslog
<EOF>

   (The <EOF> marker above represents the single trailing newline.)

2. /home/user/backup/operation.log  
   Exact byte-for-byte content:
files_to_backup.sorted.txt generated.
<EOF>

Directory permissions: /home/user/backup/ is user-writable (0700).  
No other files in /home/user/backup/ are relevant for grading.
name: task_000000_0a756d04
